statement.markdown: Given a sorted array and an element, find the index of the element using Binary Search. If the element is not present, return the index where it should be inserted to maintain sorted order. Consider edge cases of empty array, array with one element, duplicate elements, and elements at the boundary
statement.notes: Implement Binary search considering edge cases.

examples.explanation: Target 0 exists in the array at index 2
Target 6 should be inserted at index 3 to maintain sorted order

hints.text: Start with the first and last indices of the array. For each iteration, calculate the middle index and compare the middle element with the target

editorial.title: Correct Implementation of Binary Search
editorial.intuition: Correctly implementing binary search considering edge cases
editorial.optimalSolution: Implement binary search with edge cases
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: Proof of correctness for the optimal solution

functionSignature.python: def searchInsert(self, nums: list[int], target: int) -> int;
